# Intel Architectures in Detail

Intel matters in inference both as the CPU host architecture and as an
accelerator ecosystem. This chapter receives deeper treatment than a simple
vendor survey.

## Chapter Job

Explain Intel-relevant inference architecture from CPU features to accelerator
placement, software stack, and deployment tradeoffs.

## CPU-Side Topics

- Xeon server CPU role in inference nodes.
- NUMA topology and memory channels.
- PCIe lanes and accelerator attachment.
- AMX and CPU inference acceleration.
- Host-side tokenization, scheduling, networking, and preprocessing.
- CPU-only inference workloads and when they are reasonable.

## Accelerator-Side Topics

- Gaudi-class accelerator positioning.
- HBM, on-package memory, and network fabric concepts.
- Collective communication and scale-out implications.
- Software stack and framework integration.
- Precision support and compiler/runtime path.

## What to Compare Against

- NVIDIA GPU-based serving.
- AMD GPU-based serving.
- CPU-only serving.
- Specialized dataflow or LPU-style systems.

## Publication Note

This chapter should be written from official Intel architecture sources,
validated benchmark reports, and hands-on experiments. Treat secondary claims as
leads, not facts.
